evidence: protein_analysis_utils.py filter_and_compare() returns tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.Series] — consistent with actual return paths; always returns a tuple
protein_analysis_utils.py calc_min_cterm_distance() returns float | None — the function returns None when no domain found or length is zero; the annotation is accurate

protein_analysis_utils.py compare_categories() return annotation is pd.Series | pd.DataFrame — the Union return type is correct but callers must check which type they received; a narrower API would be preferable
IDR/idr_analysis.py fetch_sequence() -> str | None: accurately annotated; returns None on any exception
IDR/idr_analysis.py aiupred_summary() -> dict[str, float | None]: accurate; returns {'aiupred_mean_score': None, 'aiupred_disordered_fraction': None} when scores is empty

IDR/idr_analysis.py run_aiupred_local() -> dict[str, list[float]]: returns {} on failure — the return type annotation claims dict[str, list[float]] but returns {} (empty dict) on failure; callers that index into the result may get KeyError if they don't check

IDR/nterm_analysis.py compute_kappa() and compute_omega() return 0.0 for edge cases — the return type is float, accurate, but the semantics of 0.0 ('undefined' vs 'zero asymmetry') is conflated; no docstring note distinguishes these
app/app.py server() has no return type annotation; Shiny convention allows this but inconsistent with the rest of the module

IDR/nterm_analysis.py plot_sequence_logo() return annotation uses string forward reference 'matplotlib.axes.Axes' with # type: ignore[name-defined] — technically correct for optional matplotlib dependency but opaque

strengths: Core utility functions in protein_analysis_utils.py have accurate return type annotations
IDR/idr_analysis.py consistently uses str | None return types for functions that can fail
Docstrings in protein_analysis_utils.py and IDR/idr_analysis.py accurately describe Args and Returns
contract_coherence.issue_character: The main contract gap is run_aiupred_local() and run_mobidb_lite_local() returning {} (empty dict) on failure while their type annotation promises dict[str, list[float]] or dict[str, Any] — the empty dict is contractually valid but semantically a failure signal that is not part of the declared contract.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: Most function contracts are accurate. The primary issue is the use of empty dict as a failure sentinel in tool-invocation functions — the return type annotation is technically valid (empty dict satisfies dict[str, X]) but the semantic contract (empty means failed, non-empty means success) is implicit and undocumented. The compare_categories Union return type is a minor ergonomic issue. Overall contract quality is adequate, scoring in the upper-middle range.

issues.summary: run_aiupred_local() and run_mobidb_lite_local() return {} on failure, conflating 'empty result' with 'tool invocation failed' in their return contract
evidence: def run_aiupred_local(...) -> dict[str, list[float]]: ... return {} when dir missing or lib failed
def run_mobidb_lite_local(...) -> dict[str, Any]: ... return {} on all failure paths
Callers check 'if sequence:' but do not have a way to distinguish 'ran successfully but produced no scores' from 'failed to run'
issues.suggestion: Add a docstring note that {} signals invocation failure, and/or use Optional[dict] return type with None meaning failed — or add a 'success' key to the return dict; at minimum document the contract explicitly

issues.summary: compare_categories() return type pd.Series | pd.DataFrame is determined by the group_by parameter, but the annotation does not reflect this dependency
evidence: def compare_categories(..., group_by: str | None = None, ...) -> pd.Series | pd.DataFrame:
Returns pd.Series when group_by is None; returns pd.DataFrame when group_by is provided
Callers cannot know the return type without reading the implementation
issues.suggestion: Use @overload to provide two typed signatures: one for group_by=None returning pd.Series, one for group_by=str returning pd.DataFrame

root_causes: Empty dict failure sentinel is a common defensive pattern in research code that avoids raising exceptions; the semantic ambiguity is a side effect
compare_categories Union return was acceptable for research use but becomes a contract issue when the function is part of a public API
